You're doing $5k/month in work. Your boss takes $1k. You see $4k.

With BOSS, you do the same $5k/month. You keep $4k. We handle the phone, booking, website, payments. You show up and work.

The math is simple. The catch: you need to actually want to run your own thing.

*Results vary.*

Share this with someone still splitting their paycheck.

Here's the math nobody talks about:

You do a $500 job. Your boss takes 40-50%. You pocket $250-$300.

With BOSS, you do that same $500 job. We handle the website, phone, booking, payments. You keep $400.

That's $100 extra per job. Do 4 jobs a week? That's $400 more in your pocket every single week. $1,600 a month. Just from keeping what you actually earned.

The catch: You do the work. We don't. But that's the point—you're already doing the work. Might as well own it.

But here's what's actually happening. That missed call becomes someone else's job. The customer calls your competitor because you didn't pick up. And this isn't just happening once or twice. It's happening all week long.
